growth_records.strain: 2740-80 (wild-type, nonpathogenic environmental isolate)
growth_records.cite: Dillon et al. Genome-Wide Biases in the Rate and Molecular Spectrum of Spontaneous Mutations in Vibrio cholerae and Vibrio fischeri. Molecular Biology and Evolution (2017). DOI:10.1093/molbev/msw224. [PMC5854121]
growth_records.snippet: each of which was propagated for 217 days...Vc_wt Gen. per Line 6,453...plates were then incubated for 24 h at 28 or 37, and the daily generations per colony were calculated from the number of viable cells in each repres
growth_records.notes: Calculated mu: Table 1 gives Vc-wt mean total generations per lineage = 6453 over the stated 217-day propagation (single-cell bottleneck restreaking, colonies incubated 24 h/cycle at 37C on TSA). generations/day = 6453/217 = 29.74; mu (h^-1) = ln2 x 29.74/24 = 0.86 h^-1; doubling_time_h = 24/29.74 = 0.81 h. Colony-based average growth rate over the whole MA experiment, not a liquid growth curve; medium confidence.

growth_records.cite: Dillon et al. Genome-Wide Biases in the Rate and Molecular Spectrum of Spontaneous Mutations in Vibrio cholerae and Vibrio fischeri. Molecular Biology and Evolution (2017). DOI:10.1093/molbev/msw224. [PMC5854121]
growth_records.snippet: each of which was propagated for 43 days...Vc_mut Gen. per Line 1,254...generations of growth per day declined over the course of each MA experiment, particularly in the mutator lineages.
growth_records.notes: Calculated mu: Table 1 Vc-mut mean total generations per lineage = 1254 over stated 43-day mutator-experiment propagation (24 h/cycle, 37C, TSA). generations/day = 1254/43 = 29.16; mu = ln2 x 29.16/24 = 0.84 h^-1; doubling_time_h = 24/29.16 = 0.82 h. Experiment-average rate over the mutator MA experiment; medium confidence.

growth_records.notes: In vivo growth rate (mean ± SD) of Vibrio in the larval zebrafish intestine, measured from light-sheet fluorescence microscopy (LSFM) image-based abundance time series (not a defined lab culture medium; host = larval zebrafish reared/imaged at 28°C). Value reported directly by authors, no calculation performed by curator.
